business.headline: Dubai caught in geopolitical storm. Will it hurt its billionaire-friendly image?According to the latest Henley & Partners Private Wealth Migration Report, at least 9,800 millionaires were projected to move to the UAE in 2025 alone.

India trims gas supply to industries after Qatar halts LNG production: ReportQatar halted its LNG production on Monday as Iran continued strikes in the Gulf in response to Israeli and US attacks. The situation has disrupted energy shipments and pushed up global fuel prices and shipping costs.

Gold jumps nearly Rs 5,000: Will prices continue to rise amid the US-Iran war?The yellow metal is shining once again as geopolitical uncertainty fuels fresh buying interest. As the US-Iran war unsettles global markets, investors are wondering, will gold continue its upward climb from here?

Why is stock market closed today when people are celebrating Holi on March 4?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) have declared March 3 as the official trading holiday for Holi 2026 in their annual calendar.

EPFO interest stays at 8.25%: Why has the rate not increased this year?At a time when households are watching every rupee, even a small rise in EPF returns would have felt welcome. Instead, the rate remains at 8.25%. Here's why the retirement body has chosen stability over a hike.

Oil climbs for third day as US-Israel conflict with Iran threatens supplyThe main concern in the market remains the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil and gas supply passes.

Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

European gas futures jump 42% after Qatar halts LNG amid Middle East conflictEuropean natural gas prices have surged sharply due to the halt in LNG supplies caused by Middle East conflict. This disruption highlights Europe's growing energy vulnerability amid geopolitical tensions.

QatarEnergy to halt LPG production as Middle East tensions deepenThe crisis entered its third day as Iran widened its retaliation following joint US–Israeli strikes that killed its Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with several senior military and political figures.

If 50% of insurers offer mental health cover, why is usage below 20%?While it is encouraging that mental health cover is becoming more common, the low usage rate raises questions. Are employees fully aware of what is available to them? Do they know how to access these services? Or are they still hesitant to seek help?
EPFO's interest stays at 8.25% for FY26The last change in the rate came in 2024, when it was raised from 8.15% to 8.25% for 2023-24. Since then, there has been no further increase or reduction.
Sensex ends 1,048 lower, Nifty below 24,900; RIL down 2%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,048.34 points to close at 80,238.85, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 312.95 points to end at 24,865.70.

From LPG prices to UPI rules: 3 key March 2026 changes that could impact your budgetMarch 2026 has brought a few important changes that could affect your monthly budget. From LPG cylinder prices to new UPI rules and railway updates, here are three key changes you should know about.

Strait of Hormuz is buzzing again and why it matters to the global economyThe Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway between Iran and Oman that connects the Gulf to the Arabian Sea. On a typical day, ships carrying oil equal to about one-fifth of global demand pass through it.

Rs 9 lakh crore gone as brutal Dalal Street crash rattles investorsThe sell-off on Dalal Street has erased over Rs 9 lakh crore in investor wealth, dragging BSE's total market capitalisation down from nearly Rs 463 lakh crore to around Rs 454 lakh crore during intraday trade.

Bitcoin at $66,000 mark: 3 things crypto investors must know as Iran–US tensions riseBitcoin is hovering around the $66,000 level as global markets reel from rising Iran–US tensions. However, the key question remains: what's next?

Sensex nosedives 1,500 points today: Should investors be worried?Markets are sliding under Middle East tensions, surging crude, US–Iran hostilities and fears of trade blockages. Fundamentals look steady for now, but if the conflict deepens, is this only the first shock?

Gold jumps Rs 6,000, silver soars Rs 10,000: How Iran–US tensions affect your moneyGold and silver surged as Iran–US tensions drove investors towards safer assets. The big question is, what does this mean for your money?

RVNL, IRFC down up to 5%: Why are these railway stocks falling today?RVNL was down 5.74% to Rs 298.20 at around 12:03 pm, while IRFC shares were down 4.65% to Rs 98.74. The weakness in these names comes at a time when sentiment across domestic equities has turned cautious as global risk appetite faded and benchmark indices weakened.

L&T share price tumbles over 5%. Why is the stock falling today?L&T's slide highlights how surging crude and geopolitical uncertainty are unsettling infrastructure stocks, prompting investors to reassess cost risks and project visibility until conditions stabilise in conflict-hit regions.

entertainment.headline: Anubhav Sinha on Deepika Padukone's 8-hour shift stance: Resolve disputes privately Filmmaker Anubhav Sinha weighed in on fixed working hours in Bollywood, a conversation that was started by actor Deepika Padukone. He advocated for professional agreements between actors and producers.

Saiyami Kher reviews Taapsee Pannu's Assi: One of the most important films Saiyami Kher praised Anubhav Sinha's Assi, saying the Taapsee Pannu-starrer "refuses to leave" her even a week after watching it. She called it one of the most important films made in a long time.

I was tortured: BB 17's Anurag Dobhal alleges mental harassment, shares ordeal Bigg Boss 17's Anurag Dobhal claimed that he has been the target of mental harassment since 2025. He cited loss of family and finances, and issues concerning personal safety.

Harry Styles turns Manchester show into explosive Netflix special. Check details Harry Styles announced that his upcoming concert film, One Night in Manchester, will debut on Netflix in March, featuring the first live performance of his new album Kiss All The Time. Disco, Occasionally.

Actor Suraj Venjaramoodu calls Rajinikanth magician, talks about working in Jailer 2 Actor Suraj Venjaramoodu shared his thoughts on collaborating with Superstar Rajinikanth in Jailer 2, calling it a milestone. This is his first-ever collaboration with Rajinikanth.

Heart of gold: Karan Aujla's kind gesture for Mumbai auto drivers wins praise Punjabi singer Karan Aujla recently won hearts after he settled pending fines for two Mumbai auto rickshaw drivers, totalling Rs 22,000, ahead of his concert in the city.

Rajpal Yadav: Priyadarshan, David Dhawan, RGV ensured there was no work shortage Rajpal Yadav expressed gratitude for consistently getting work in Bollywood. He credited filmmakers like Priyadarshan, David Dhawan and Ram Gopal Varma for repeatedly collaborating with him.

Composer Anirudh talks about Rajini-Kamal reunion, says dream collab includes SRK Anirudh Ravichander opens up about composing for the Rajini–Kamal reunion, choosing retro sound over a mass template for the film's popular number, Aaja Raja, and the dream collaboration that's still on his bucket list.

Yash's Toxic has already collected Rs 600 crore, claims Tamil producer Film producer Dr G Dhananjayan discussed how deliberate casting, marketing, and release strategies have contributed to the substantial pre-release earnings of Yash's Toxic: A Fairy Tale for Grown-Ups.

Esha Gupta back in India from Abu Dhabi amid Middle East conflict, posts note Esha Gupta, who was stranded in Abu Dhabi amid Middle East conflict, has safely returned to India. She shared her experience of the crisis, expressing gratitude to the governments of India and the UAE for their assistance.

Rashmika Mandanna goes down on one knee for Vijay at sangeet, shares dance pics Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda shared a romantic dance at their sangeet night in Udaipur. The surprise moments and family performances added warmth and joy to their wedding festivities.

Kartik Aaryan's Sonu Ke Titu Ki Sweety to re-release in theatres after Holi The 2018 romantic comedy Sonu Ke Titu Ki Sweety is set to return to theatres across India. The film played a defining role in actor Kartik Aaryan's career and also starred Nushrratt Bharuccha and Sunny Singh.

Honey Singh assures fans of his safety in Dubai amid Middle East tensions Yo Yo Honey Singh reassured fans he is safe in Dubai amid rising tensions in the Middle East. The rapper shared a studio video as regional hostilities involving Iran, Israel and the US intensified.

Ahaan Panday underwent one of the most painful surgeries after Saiyaara release Ahaan Panday revealed that he underwent major shoulder surgery soon after the release of his debut film Saiyaara. He said he kept the ordeal private as he prepared for his next project with Ali Abbas Zafar.

Priyanka Chopra hopes good wins over evil on Holika Dahan amid Middle East conflict Amid the US-Iran conflict, Priyanka Chopra marked Holika Dahan with a message of hope, acknowledging global unrest as several Indian celebrities remain stranded in Dubai due to travel disruptions.

Laughs, tears and surprises: Inside Rashmika Mandanna-Vijay Deverakonda's sangeet Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared heartfelt moments during their sangeet ceremony. Their joyous celebrations featured emotional speeches, romantic dances and stunning outfits, setting the tone for their wedding.

Dalimb first look: Jitendra Kumar takes on darker role for psychological thriller The first look of Jitendra Kumar's psychological thriller Dalimb was unveiled by ZEE5 on Holi, marking his shift into a darker and more intense role.

Rashmika-Vijay announce scholarships to students in his hometown after wedding Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda visited his native village in Telangana, Thummanpet, to perform post-wedding pujas and visit his new home. They pledged scholarships for Class 9 and 10 students across 44 government schools to support their education.

The Kerala Story 2 box office Day 4: Film sees dip, earns Rs 12.8 crore The Kerala Story 2: Goes Beyond, which released amid controversy, witnessed a significant drop compared to the first installment. The Hindi film's total earnings now stand at Rs 12.8 crore after four days.

general news.headline: Toll from US-Israel strikes on Iran rises to 787 amid escalation in Middle EastThe death toll from the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, which began on February 28, has risen to 787. The strikes, which resulted in the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, has triggered retaliatory attacks by Tehran on US and Israeli bases across the Gulf.

Spy dental chip to IRGC mole: Theories on how Mossad pinpointed Khamenei for strikeIran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed in a US-Israeli air strike. Reports suggest that it was Israeli intelligence agency Mossad that pinpointed Khamenei's location for the hit. Now, wild theories are circulating about how Mossad might have got the Iranian leader's location.

Live: Israel indicates no ground op in Iran, says 'not practical'US-Israel Iran Strikes Live Updates: The death toll from US-Israel strikes on Iran rose to 787 on Tuesday as the conflict entered its fourth day, with escalating attacks across the Middle East. The United States carried out air raids targeting Iran's government and military leadership, while Tehran retaliated by striking American bases and interests in the Gulf, widening the crisis. US President Donald Trump declined to rule out deploying ground troops, saying Washington is prepared for a prolonged campaign. Iran, however, said it would not bow to pressure but remains open to negotiations. Tehran also announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, warning that any vessel attempting to pass through the strategic waterway would be set ablaze. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates on the escalating military conflict in the Middle East.

Has India quietly chosen a side in US-Israel's war with Iran?The messaging from India on the US-Israel conflict with Iran seems to indicate a careful review of its decades-long balancing act. Neither has India condemned the strikes on Iran, a strategic partner, nor spoken on the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Has India already chosen a side in the war?

Was Ayatollah Khamenei India's friend?Ayatollah Ali Khamenei repeatedly commented on India's internal matters – from Kashmir to the CAA, Delhi riots and comparisons with Gaza – prompting diplomatic protests from New Delhi.

Why Pakistan set its own house on fire just before Iran warHandcrafted ByOur Editors The war thrust upon Afghanistan by Asim Munir and Shehbaz Sharif, the unchecked protests, and the killing of protesters at the US consulate in Karachi have the one same motive. Deny Trump the pound of flesh he might seek during the Iran war.

China's HQ-9B air defence fails twice in a year: After Op Sindoor, it's Iran nowChina's HQ-9B air-defence system, advertised as a flagship military hardware, is now under scrutiny after failures in Iran and Pakistan, raising questions about its combat credibility. The latest failure during US-Israeli strikes might weaken Beijing's military export appeal.

Blood Moon 2026 LIVE: Rare lunar eclipse rises in India. WATCHThe world is set to witness the first lunar eclipse of the year today. The Moon will turn fiery red. Follow this live blog on India Today Science for the latest updates on the lunar eclipse, also known as the Blood Moon. Find the exact chandra grahan timings in India and tips on how to watch it.

Time, Tide & Tamil Part 29| Era of Scams & Defiance, Emergency Eclipse & Fall of the AxeBy late 1972, the Dravidian movement had moved from a symbiotic partnership to a scorched-earth rivalry. The expulsion of MG Ramachandran (MGR) from the DMK was not merely a party internal matter; it was a fatal fracture of faith.

Claude goes offline twice in 24 hours after US labels Anthropic as supply riskClaude went offline twice within 24 hours after the US Government labelled Anthropic as a "supply chain risk" mandating all federal agencies to stop using the chatbot immediately.

New Voter Atlas launched to track Indian-American voters, counter racial rhetoricIndian-origin US political strategist Anang Mittal has created a new freely accessible online platform compiling publicly available data on the Indian-American community. Titled the Indian American Voter Atlas, it seeks to provide policymakers with a consolidated database on various aspects of Indian-origin citizens in the US to help understand their civic and political influence, as well as counter-racist rhetoric.

Pak strikes Bagram Air Base in Afghanistan, satellite pics capture damageSatellite photos obtained by The New York Times show that the Pakistani military hit Bagram Air Base, signalling a sharp escalation in its war with Afghanistan and hitting one of the Taliban administration's most valued military assets. Bagram once served as the largest US military base in Afghanistan.

Laughs, tears and surprises: Inside Rashmika Mandanna-Vijay Deverakonda's sangeetVijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared heartfelt moments during their sangeet ceremony. Their joyous celebrations featured emotional speeches, romantic dances and stunning outfits, setting the tone for their wedding.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

Can't evacuate, make own plans: US Embassy in Israel to Americans as Iran war ragesThe embassy stated it is not currently in a position to evacuate or directly assist private U.S. citizens in departing Israel, urging individuals to make their own security plans.

Magnitude 4.3 earthquake hits Iran's Gerash amid escalating Israeli-US attacksThe earthquake comes amid raging regional hostilities as the US and Israel have escalated attacks against Iran. There were no immediate reports of significant damage or casualties.

Watch: Las Vegas Sphere turns blood-red ahead of rare lunar eclipseThe Las Vegas Sphere has turned into a giant Blood Moon to mark the total lunar eclipse. Learn why the Moon turns red and how to watch this celestial event.

Road trip to Oman, high airfares: Indians stuck in UAE amid Iran war share ordealAs the US-Israel-Iran conflict entered its fourth day, several Indians stranded in the UAE said they travelled for six hours by road to Oman to catch their flights. While the situation on the ground remained calm in Dubai, they described growing fear due to widespread airspace closures across the Gulf.

Rs 1 crore package in placement, but what is the real take-home?Rs 1 crore packages in campus placements are often headline grabbers but not common. Most graduates receive modest salaries with complex compensation structures affecting take-home pay.

US Embassies in Saudi, Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan shut as Iran conflict escalatesThe United States has closed multiple embassies and ordered the evacuation of non-emergency personnel across parts of the Gulf after Iranian drone attacks targeted the US Embassy in Riyadh.

Kidulting this Holi: Grown-ups spend 'adult money' on super-cool pichkarisNot just kids, but grown-ups are splurging, spending what they jokingly call their 'adult money' on the coolest designs they can find.

Is BJP's Suvendu Adhikari planning to take on Mamata in her Kolkata bastion?The 2021 West Bengal Assembly Elections saw a high-stakes battle in the Nandigram constituency, as Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee unsuccessfully took on turncoat and BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari. In the 2026 election, Adhikari could bring the fight to Mamata by challenging her in Bhabanipur, the TMC chief's Kolkata bastion.

Tony Fadell says iPod is back as users have again started using itTony Fadell says the iPod is quietly making a comeback as users rediscover the distraction-free music player. Instead of streaming apps, many are turning to old iPods for a more focused listening experience.

Why Congress is pushing hard for Maharashtra's lone Rajya Sabha seatWith 72 Rajya Sabha seats falling vacant this year, the Congress party faces a crucial test of numbers that could determine whether it retains the Leader of Opposition (LoP) position. With the retirement of senior Congress leaders shortly and the party falling short of the threshold numbers to fetch the LoP post, negotiations have begun.

sports.headline: Gautam Gambhir salutes Rinku Singh's courage for turning up after personal tragedy Gautam Gambhir delivered an emotional tribute to Rinku Singh in a team huddle, praising him for rejoining the squad days after his father's death ahead of India's clash against the West Indies.

Abhishek Sharma needs to change mindset: EX-IND batter slams opener after WI flopshow T20 World Cup: Former India batter Manoj Tiwary has asked Abhishek Sharma to tweak his mindset a bit after his slump in form continued in the T20 World Cup 2026. Abhishek was dismissed for 10 off 11 balls in Kolkata.

Father time for Ro-Ko: Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli embrace dad duties, videos go viral Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli are embracing the roles of doting fathers perfectly as the duo were seen spending time with their sons, Ahaan and Akaay, respectively.

Travis Head rejects claims Australia lacked commitment in ICC tournament ICC T20 World Cup: Travis Head has hit back at the public narrative suggesting the Australian team crashed out in the group stage because they do not care as much about the format.

Will India unleash Jasprit Bumrah in powerplay vs ENG? Sunil Gavaskar weighs in India should unleash Jasprit Bumrah early in the powerplay against England's top order to break their batting backbone, says Sunil Gavaskar ahead of the T20 World Cup semifinal in Mumbai.

SA vs NZ: Why New Zealand must be wary of in-form Aiden Markram in semi-final T20 World Cup: Captain Aiden Markram's aggressive starts will be key for South Africa in the first semi-final against New Zealand at Kolkata's Eden Gardens. Markram's sensational run as opener has powered South Africa's unbeaten run.

Flamengo sack Filipe Luis, line up Leonardo Jardim as his leading replacement Clube de Regatas do Flamengo have parted ways with head coach Filipe Luis after a series of underwhelming results, with Portuguese tactician Leonardo Jardim now emerging as the frontrunner to assume charge ahead of the Carioca final.

Afridi slams son-in-law Shaheen after T20 World Cup exit: Repeats same mistakes Shahid Afridi has slammed his son-in-law and pacer Shaheen after the Pakistan team's exit from the T20 World Cup 2026. Shaheen was expensive during the tournament and failed to inspire the Pakistan pace attack.

ICC unveils Pycroft, Srinath as match referees for T20 World Cup 2026 semifinals The ICC has named Javagal Srinath and Andy Pycroft as match referees for the T20 World Cup 2026 semifinals in Kolkata and Mumbai, confirming the full list of officials for the knockout clashes.

Zaheer Khan conducts specialised red-ball camp for fast bowlers at BCCI CoE Former India fast bowler Zaheer Khan led a specialised red-ball training camp on Monday for the high-performance monitoring group at the Board of Control for Cricket in India Centre of Excellence (CoE).

PV Sindhu returns to India after Dubai ordeal, to miss All England Championships Two-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu has returned home to Bengaluru after her ordeal in Dubai, where she was left stranded in the city due to the middle east war. Sindhu will miss the All England Championships, which will begin on March 3.

Atiqa dazzles in Europe, becomes 1st Indian to get podium in COTFA seriesAtiqa Mir made history by finishing third overall in the opening round of the Champions of the Future Academy karting series in Europe. The 11-year-old, racing in the OKNJ junior category, delivered strong performances across the weekend despite penalties and setbacks.

14 Pakistan players in The Hundred auction list: Will Indian owners buy them?Haris Rauf is among the 14 Pakistani men's cricketers in the 247-player auction list for this year's Hundred, amid reports that Indian-owned franchises could refrain from signing players from across the border.

'Big game pacer' Jasprit Bumrah backed to deliver for India in semi-finalICC T20 World Cup: Australian fast-bowling great Glenn McGrath has termed Jasprit Bumrah a big-game player, backing the star pacer to deliver for India in the semi-final against England at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium on March 5.

Virat Kohli will forever be RCB's icon and hero: Mo BobatRoyal Challengers Bengaluru director of cricket Mo Bobat has praised Virat Kohli, saying the former India captain will always remain RCB's icon and hero after spending nearly two decades with the franchise in the Indian Premier League.

The ace who loves a joke: The two sides of Arshdeep SinghT20 World Cup 2026: Arshdeep Singh brings laughter to the dressing room and calm to India's toughest moments. Behind the easy smile is a bowler who has quietly earned the team's deepest trust.

Women's football team sent undersized jerseys, forced to buy local kitsDays before their AFC Women's Asia Cup opener, the Indian team was hit by a jersey fiasco as the All India Football Federation (AIFF) made a big blunder by sending the squad the wrong kits.

Well experienced in India conditions, New Zealand ready for SA challenge at EdenT20 World Cup: New Zealand are set to take on South Africa in the semi-final 1 of the T20 World Cup 2026. New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell is confident that their experience in Indian conditions will keep them well prepared going into the clash against South Africa.

Wounded Barcelona ready to make 'impossible, possible' against Atletico MadridBarcelona face a daunting 0-4 deficit in the Copa del Rey semi-final second leg against Atletico Madrid. Hansi Flick has called upon his team to make the impossible, possible.

West Indies, Zimbabwe stranded in Kolkata due to Middle East conflictThe West Indies cricket team are stranded in Kolkata after their T20 World Cup exit. The development comes due to airspace closures caused by tensions between Israel and Iran in the Middle East.

Captain? Won't even keep him in the team: Afridi lashes out at Pakistan playerFormer Pakistan captain Shahid Afridi remains furious at the team. He has lashed out at one of the players and said that, let alone captaincy, he does not even deserve a place in the team.

Is Sehwag unprofessional? Fans call out ex-batter over viral Miller remarksFormer India batter Virender Sehwag's comment about David Miller has gone viral on the internet. Many cricket fans are calling Sehwag unprofessional due to the nature of his admission.

The IND vs PAK handshake row: What do the outsiders think ft. Bavuma, VaughanTemba Bavuma says the India–Pakistan no-handshake gesture does not look good for cricket, while admitting he is not fully aware of the political context behind the decision.

Mohammad Amir is not wrong. India are yet to show up as a teamSanju Samson's unbeaten 97 rescued India from T20 World Cup elimination against West Indies, sparing careers. Yet, batting woes, Bumrah-dependent bowling, and butterfingers fielding expose deep cracks heading into the England semi-final.

Enjoyed the cupcake ad: South Africa coach takes dig at World Cup promoSouth Africa head coach Shukri Conrad has revealed that the team was well aware of the cupcake advertisement made by T20 World Cup broadcaster Star Sports. Conrad took a sly dig at the ad ahead of their semi-final clash against New Zealand.

FIFA chief calls for red cards to players covering mouths after Prestianni racism rowFIFA president Gianni Infantino says players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents could face red cards if linked to racist abuse after the Vinicius Junior incident.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran's participation uncertain amid ongoing military conflictIran's football federation president remains doubtful whether the national team can fulfil its World Cup fixtures in the United States after U.S.-Israeli strikes heightened regional tensions.

The night Sanju Samson fulfilled his prophecyIND vs WI, T20 World Cup: Sanju Samson played the best innings of his career to guide India to the semi-final of the T20 World Cup 2026. Samson, who debuted for India in 2015, finally fulfilled his prophecy.

Pakistan board fines players Rs 16 lakh each after T20 World Cup failure: ReportPakistan Cricket Board has reportedly imposed a Rs 16 lakh fine on each player following their early exit from the 2026 T20 World Cup. Pakistan failed to go past the Super 8 stage in the tournament.

Justice by Samson: Irfan Pathan says wicketkeeper reminded everyone of KohliT20 World Cup: Irfan Pathan said that Sanju Samson reminded everyone of Virat Kohli with his knock in the India vs West Indies clash on Sunday, March 1. Samson's knock helped India reach the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
